items.title: The dead fish waste disaster that Tasmania failed to plan for

children.content: High-volume salmon mortalities last year swamped Tasmania's waste streams as companies scrambled to find somewhere to accept them, documents show. There are now concerns the state remains unprepared for future mass deaths.

gallery.altText: A large amount of fish are laid out in a trench
a skip bin with salmon carcasses

thumbnail.altText: A large amount of fish are laid out in a trench
a skip bin with salmon carcasses

items.title: Hawthorn 'extremely disappointed' by AFL's call to end Tasmanian partnership

children.content: Hawthorn is less than pleased after being forced to cease playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the Devils' AFL entry.

image.altText: Jai Newcombe holding a football in his right hand

items.title: Apology given for 'appalling' display of autopsy body parts without consent

children.content: A formal apology is given in Tasmania's parliament for the past practice of taking human specimens from autopsies and displaying them in a museum without the knowledge of family members.

image.altText: Slices of body parts covered in Perspex and put on display on walls.

items.title: It's time for Eric Abetz to tackle Tasmania's debt. How far will he go?

children.content: The lead-up to state budget day is normally a time for flashy funding announcements. But as Tasmania stares down the barrel of $7 billion of debt, things are looking a bit bleaker.

image.altText: A politician speaking to journalists in a room.

items.title: Man charged with murder following discovery of body in Hobart home

children.content: A 56-year-old man has been charged with murder after the body of a woman was found in a Hobart residence over the weekend.

image.altText: Lissadell Court Tasmania Police 4

items.title: Stranded driver, students among 10 rescues during heavy rainfall in NSW and Qld

children.content: Almost 50 high school students and teachers have been rescued after becoming stranded by floodwater at a national park in the Scenic Rim while on a school camp.
Earlier, near the New South Wales and Queensland border, crews worked to rescue a person stuck in a vehicle at Numinbah Valley near Murwillumbah.
NSW SES volunteers and emergency service partners have responded to 197 incidents since Monday afternoon.

gallery.altText: A mountain range in regional Queensland.

thumbnail.altText: A mountain range in regional Queensland.

items.title: Trump says he has postponed a planned new strike on Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump says an attack on Iran that was "scheduled for tomorrow" has been postponed at the request of Gulf state leaders.

image.altText: Donald Trump with hands folded looks to the side

items.title: ASX jumps more than 1 per cent in rebound rally

children.content: The local share market is rebounding from Monday's heavy selling, as shares in Tuas make back some of their heavy losses. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: How to spot the 'psychosis' red flags in an 'AI relationship'

children.content: Intensive chatbot use may be amplifying dangerous and harmful delusions for some users. Here are some of the "red flags" for "AI psychosis".

items.title: The parts of Victoria adding the biggest share to disaster damage bills

children.content: At least $57 billion worth of infrastructure in Victoria is vulnerable to more frequent and severe bushfires, floods and extreme heat. This figure is expected to grow.

items.title: Married at First Sight UK off air after rape allegations

children.content: British broadcaster Channel 4 removes all previous seasons of Married at First Sight after rape allegations come to light.

image.altText: A newly married couple hold hands at their wedding.

items.title: What Kylie Minogue learned from Michael Hutchence and Nick Cave

children.content: Australia's reigning Queen of Pop discusses her relationships with Nick Cave and the late Michael Hutchence, and being tapped to headline the AFL grand final's pre-game entertainment.

items.title: More jet fuel secured from China, Albanese says

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese announces the federal government has secured 600,000 barrels of jet fuel from China. Follow live.

image.altText: Close up of Anthony Albanese wearing a white hard hat and glasses.

items.title: Boomers star Joe Ingles thrilled to come 'home' in coup NBL signing

children.content: The legendary Boomers and NBA star will return to Australia's NBL for the first time since 2009, signing with Melbourne United for two years.

image.altText: Joe Ingles smiles with a towel around his neck

items.title: Money stress causing tension in your relationship? This might help

children.content: While financial tension between partners isn't always about money, many people clash over values, spending habits and how they respond to financial pressure.

items.title: Two German film students track down their stolen movie in new doco

children.content: What do you do when your stolen film wins a slew of awards for the thief? In the case of these filmmakers, you travel across the globe to hunt him down.

items.title: 'Two years to live': Why we used our super for weight loss surgery

children.content: Weight loss surgery has become an increasingly common option for people grappling with obesity — and many are funding it using retirement savings.

items.title: Flight path granny flat ban lifted in parts of Sydney's west

children.content: The NSW government will permit secondary dwellings such as granny flats in some areas affected by noise from Western Sydney International Airport.

image.altText: A dual occupancy home with a white mailbox that has two openings that read "A" and "B".

items.title: Elon Musk loses lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman

children.content: The Tesla boss accused OpenAI of wrongfully trying to enrich investors and insiders at the nonprofit's expense, and failing to prioritise AI's safety.

image.altText: A composite image showing Elon Musk and Sam Altman's faces.

items.title: Two men arrested over stunt at Punch the monkey's enclosure

children.content: One of the men was detained by a zookeeper and arrested after allegedly climbing a fence and breaking into the enclosure.

image.altText: A baby Japanese macaque next to an Ikea stuffed orangutan toy.

items.title: Tomic edges closer to French Open amid Aussie qualifying nightmare

children.content: Bernard Tomic is just two wins from reaching his first main draw of the French Open since 2019 after being the only Australian to enjoy success in the qualifiers.

image.altText: tennis player Bernard Tomic hits a one handed backhand shot

items.title: Historic flour mill walls reduced to rubble after failed heritage bid

children.content: In the face of protests, the remnants of a historic flour mill in Mount Gambier are now a pile of crumbled stone. The site will be developed into a multi-storey hotel complex and townhouses.

items.title: Woman dies after being hit by ute on Pacific Motorway

children.content: Police say initial enquiries indicate a female was out of her vehicle when she was struck.

image.altText: A highway blocked off with emergency responders

items.title: 'Morale is low': Grocery, butcher's set alight in Melbourne shopping strip

children.content: Fire crews were called to a blaze in Glenroy that gutted several shops. Locals say there have been multiple suspicious fires in the area.

image.altText: CCTV footage showing two masked men

items.title: Community fights back against caravan park development proposal

children.content: A community group in Victoria's south-east will appeal a council planning decision to approve the development of a regional caravan park, which it says would be an "absolute disaster" for the region.

items.title: Israeli forces intercept Gaza flotilla with Australians on board, organisers say

children.content: The organisers of a flotilla of ships trying to break Israel's maritime blockade of Gaza say Israel's navy is intercepting the group off the coast of Cyprus.

items.title: Bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers found in underwater cave in Maldives

children.content: The bodies of four missing Italian scuba divers who died last week have been found in an underwater cave in the Maldives.

image.altText: Side-by-side images of a mother (left) and her daughter.

items.title: A secret mouse experiment in the 1990s threatened Brisbane's water supply

children.content: Brisbane was days away from running out of drinkable water in the 1990s due to a secret mouse experiment that was kept hidden from the public … until now.

items.title: Court shutdown means justice takes much longer in Balgo

children.content: In this remote East Kimberley community, where the local court has been shut for almost two years due to security concerns, the closure of the nearest regional court from June 1 is raising concerns about justice.

image.altText: A map showing Balgo, Western Australia.

items.title: Cummins stars as Sunrisers Hyderabad book spot in IPL playoffs

children.content: Pat Cummins delivers when his team needs it most with crucial wickets as Sunrisers Hyderabad beat Chennai Super Kings.
